words.tip: Short 'e' sound.
Long 'aa' vowel.
Dual meaning — context tells you which.
Word order stays normal after 'want' (unlike 'omdat').
Verb goes to end: 'omdat ik moe ben'.
'Dus jij bent klaar' = So you're done.
'oo' = long 'oh'.
Very versatile. 'Nog een?' = Another one?
'Ik ben al klaar' = I'm already done. Also 'al' = although.
'Ik ben er bijna' = I'm almost there.
Stressing the 'mid' syllable. 'Kom onmiddellijk!' = Come immediately!
Lit. 'within short'. 'Tot binnenkort' = See you soon.
Soft 'g' both times. Also means 'happy': 'Ik ben gelukkig'.
Long 'aa'. 'Helaas kan ik niet.' = Unfortunately I can't.
Typically Belgian! 'Spijtig genoeg' = Unfortunately (lit. pityingly enough).
'Doe het zelf' = Do it yourself. Adds emphasis.
'Even wachten' = just wait. 'Kan ik even...?' = Can I just...?
'Dat is gewoon zo' = That's just how it is. Very Belgian filler word.
'Vooral in de zomer' = especially in summer.
'Echt?' = Really? Great as a reaction. 'Dat is echt leuk' = That's really fun.
Modal verb form of 'zullen'. 'Ik zal het doen' = I will do it.
